farming: Farming is the practice of cultivating crops and raising livestock for food, fiber, and other products useful to sustain life.
crop rotation: Crop rotation means growing different crops on the same land in different seasons to improve soil fertility and reduce pests.
irrigation: Irrigation is the process of supplying water to crops artificially to help them grow when rainfall is insufficient.
soil fertility: Soil fertility is the ability of soil to provide essential nutrients to plants for healthy growth.
fertilizer: Fertilizers are substances added to soil to improve plant growth by supplying nutrients such as nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium.
organic farming: Organic farming is a method of agriculture that uses natural fertilizers and pest control instead of synthetic chemicals.
pesticide: Pesticides are chemicals used to protect crops from insects, weeds, fungi, and other pests.
green revolution: The Green Revolution was a period of agricultural transformation that increased crop production through improved seeds, fertilizers, and irrigation.
harvesting: Harvesting is the process of collecting mature crops from the fields.
compost: Compost is organic material such as food waste and plant matter that decomposes and improves soil quality.
drip irrigation: Drip irrigation is a water-saving irrigation technique where water is delivered directly to the plant roots through pipes.
rainwater harvesting: Rainwater harvesting is the collection and storage of rainwater for reuse in agriculture and household needs.
crop yield: Crop yield is the amount of crop produced per unit area of land.
soil erosion: Soil erosion is the removal of the top layer of soil by wind, water, or human activities.
agriculture: Agriculture is the science and practice of cultivating soil, growing crops, and raising animals for food and resources.
livestock: Livestock refers to domesticated animals such as cows, goats, sheep, and poultry raised on farms.
dairy farming: Dairy farming is the agricultural practice of raising animals for milk production.
green manure: Green manure refers to plants grown and then plowed back into the soil to improve fertility.
kharif crops: Kharif crops are grown during the monsoon season in India, such as rice, maize, and cotton.
rabi crops: Rabi crops are grown in the winter season in India, such as wheat, mustard, and barley.
